mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future equity cash flows, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand and construction sector expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows negative predictive power with Rank IC below -0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in a 1-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases when CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4%, or the inflation threshold shows no statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple quarters, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and reduced corporate output, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in > 55% of instances when INDPRO falls > 0.5%, or the signal predictive power is indistinguishable from random chance with Rank IC below 0.005 over a 2-year window.
